reviews.review_msg: He is a nice guy and you can tell he is smart. Will he teach you anything, no. Be prepared to learn nothing. He doesn't teach, he makes the students present all class. He give way too many assignments worth like 3% or 2% but his criteria is unclear and he makes it up as he goes. And you literally have to force him to give you feedback or a grade!

Landon, seems like a nice guy. But as an instructor he is disorganized, he extremely slow grading, so by the time you get feedback your well into the next topic and it's hard to go back and figure out his vague feedback. tons of reading, and the most work I've experienced in a Grade course yet....

Lectures were relatively interesting - Landon had a dry sense of humour that not enough people appreciate. However, I did not learn anything from Landon and there is too much work in this class. Your group matters in this course since there are weekly group assignments, two group reports, and a group presentation.

worst prof at sfu... unprepared, has no idea what he's talking about.  assignments are vague and have no criteria, takes forever to mark. teaches case style but doesn't provide any supporting tools to help analyze the case. extremely slow to reply to e-mails. If you send him an e-mail after class, he won't reply until the day before the assignment

Go to class - Landon isn't the clearest or most thrilling of professors, but without his words, his slides will not be so intuitive. As there are no tutorials, but merely labs, going to class if the only obligation you have as a student. He will try his best to make the course interesting, and the exam follows what he said would be included.

Nice guy but you won't learn anything from him. You don't get feedback until the after the semester so it's impossible to know how you're doing. If you want to get your money's worth and actually learn something, take it with a different prof.
Landon is a great professor. He is a little bit dry, but overall his lectures are enjoyable and the class content interesting.

Landon is a great professor. His lectures are both informative and interesting. He puts information in a way that is easily relatable and appealing. He does have issues with organization, but it's not that big of a deal. Overall, he's a great prof that's open to feedback and is always willing to listen to your needs.
Way far behind your standard. Very boring and unclear.  Not sure if he knows how to teach

Horrible. Lecture slides literally needed 5 staples because he has too many slides and he doesn't even go through all of them. He is unhelpful and unclear. A nice guy, but finishes last on my prof list!
